metadata.baseline_design: Fair baselines — without-skill prompts contain ZERO vault/Obsidian hints, just the task

key_findings: E5 DISCRIMINATING: without-skill used LEGACY conventions (Title Case, flat tags, callouts, date/status/parent) — ignored vault root skill entirely. With-skill correctly loaded vault-style skill (kebab-case, nested tags, no callouts).

E8 PARTIALLY DISCRIMINATING: without-skill used Mermaid + UPPERCASE filename (learned from existing notes) BUT kept tags in frontmatter (vault-docs says no frontmatter tags, inline only). With-skill correctly used inline #tags, no frontmatter tags.
E3 DISCRIMINATING: without-skill created note with NO frontmatter at all — just a plain markdown file. With-skill used fallback conventions (tags, date, parent, callouts).
E9 DISCRIMINATING: without-skill used kebab-case filename (vault-stats-cli-planning.md) — vault CLAUDE.md says Title Case. With-skill correctly used Title Case.
E4 DISCRIMINATING: with-skill ran full setup protocol (validate, symlink, AGENTS.md, vault root resolution, git tracking info). Without-skill created symlink but no structured setup.
Race condition: E1, E2, E7 with-skill found notes already created by without-skill (parallel runs share fixtures)
